title: euphrasia nemorosa subsp. tatarica
intro: Euphrasia nemorosa subsp. tatarica, also known as the Tatarian eyebright, is a plant species within the family Orobanchaceae, which is a type of parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of a host plant. The medicinal properties of Euphrasia nemorosa subsp. tatarica include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been traditionally used to treat eye and respiratory infections. The health benefits of this plant are numerous, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve vision, as well as its use in treating conditions such as conjunctivitis, sinusitis, and bronchitis. Euphrasia nemorosa subsp. tatarica can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support eye and respiratory health.
